mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes that is designed for ease of deployment and management using Juju. It is ideal for users looking for a robust Kubernetes solution that integrates well with Ubuntu and provides modern metrics and monitoring capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for Juju and the potential complexity of managing charms.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
Juju is required to deploy Charmed Kubernetes. This step installs the Juju CLI on your system.

```bash

sudo snap install juju --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of Juju.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Juju controller
steps.description:
This command initializes the Juju controller, which is necessary for deploying Charmed Kubernetes.

```bash

juju bootstrap ubuntu-cloud my-k8s-controller --config controller-image=juju/juju:2.9.0

```

> Replace 'ubuntu-cloud' with your cloud provider if needed.

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Charmed Kubernetes bundle, which includes all necessary components.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes --channel 1.31/stable

```

> You can change the channel to 1.30/stable or 1.29/stable as needed.

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the deployed services to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Look for all services to be in the 'active' state.

steps.title: Configure monitoring and autoscaling
steps.description:
This step sets up monitoring and autoscaling for your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

juju add-relation prometheus-k8s:monitoring kubernetes-master:monitoring

juju config kubernetes-worker min-nodes=2 max-nodes=5

```

> Ensure Prometheus is deployed.
> Adjust the number of nodes as per your requirements.

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Charmed Kubernetes deployment and may lead to data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

juju destroy-controller my-k8s-controller --destroy-all-models

```

> This will remove all resources associated with the controller.

uninstall.title: Cleanup orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to clean up any orphaned resources that may remain after the uninstallation.

```bash

juju remove-application charmed-kubernetes

```

> This will ensure all applications related to Charmed Kubernetes are removed.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your current configuration and data. This step will upgrade your Charmed Kubernetes installation.

```bash

juju config kubernetes-master channel=1.31/stable

juju upgrade-charm kubernetes-master

juju status

```

> Change the channel to your desired version.
> This command upgrades the Kubernetes master charm.
> Verify that all services are running correctly after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju bootstrap failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to incorrect cloud credentials or network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your cloud provider credentials and ensure your network is configured correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubernetes-master not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes master may not have completed its initialization.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju status' to check the logs and ensure all dependencies are met.

**Affected versions:** 1.29, 1.30, 1.31

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'relation not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a required relation between services is missing.

**Fix:**
Ensure all necessary relations are established using 'juju add-relation'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'resource not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if a charm or resource has been removed or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the charm configuration and ensure all resources are correctly defined.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features including improved metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy charm configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure to update your charm configurations to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see all services in the 'active' state when running 'juju status'. The output should indicate that the Kubernetes cluster is fully operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a compatible cloud environment and sufficient resources for deployment.
